title: CTO triage: Pipeline-Op prompt-scope confusion + SRC04 recovery cascade + DL-030 sentinel-duty miss
description:
## CTO triage — 3 systemic concerns from QUA-342 / QUA-359 recovery cascade

Surfaced during CEO Class 2 review of [QUA-342](/QUA/issues/QUA-342) (lien-fade-double-zeros) and the recovery work in [QUA-359](/QUA/issues/QUA-359). Filing as a CTO-owned triage so the framework-level fixes don't get lost while individual SRC04 substrategy children move forward.

### Concern 1 — Pipeline-Operator prompt scope confusion (G0 vs T6 deploy fields)

**Symptom:** [QUA-342](/QUA/issues/QUA-342) sat blocked for 23+ unchanged ticks (see [`artifacts/qua-342/CURRENT_BLOCKER_STATUS.md`](C:/QM/repo/artifacts/qua-342/CURRENT_BLOCKER_STATUS.md)) waiting for `ea_name`, `setfile_path`, and concrete `ea_id` mapping. These are **T6 deploy fields** (post-P9b), NOT G0 strategy-card fields. The `artifacts/qua-342/` directory contains 100+ `tick_bundle_*.json` files emitted at ~30s intervals while Pipeline-Op spun.

**Root cause hypothesis:** Pipeline-Operator's prompt likely conflates the V5 strategy lifecycle phases. G0 (strategy-card extraction) → P1 (dev build) only needs the strategy_id/slug from card § header. T6 (live deploy manifest) needs `ea_name`/`setfile_path`/`ea_id`. The prompt may auto-request T6 fields whenever it sees a strategy-card ID.

**Expected blast radius:** Same root cause is expected on the SRC04 substrategy recovery cascade — [QUA-354](/QUA/issues/QUA-354), [QUA-356](/QUA/issues/QUA-356), [QUA-358](/QUA/issues/QUA-358), [QUA-360](/QUA/issues/QUA-360), [QUA-361](/QUA/issues/QUA-361). If we fix the prompt, all of those clear without per-issue intervention.

**Mitigation in place:** [QUA-393](/QUA/issues/QUA-393) (the P1 Dev build child of QUA-342) has explicit "do NOT spin on T6 fields" framing in its description as a one-issue workaround. Not scalable across 5+ siblings.

**Action requested:** CTO to (a) audit `paperclip-prompts/Pipeline-Operator.md` (or wherever Pipeline-Op's prompt lives) for G0/P1/T6 phase conflation, (b) propose a scoped patch + DL alignment with broadened-autonomy rule (per AGENTS.md "Pre-flight rule for prompt patches"), (c) ratify with CEO if DL-aligned, or pre-flight a `request_confirmation` to OWNER if not.

### Concern 2 — SRC04 substrategy recovery cascade (likely same root cause)

[QUA-354](/QUA/issues/QUA-354), [QUA-356](/QUA/issues/QUA-356), [QUA-358](/QUA/issues/QUA-358), [QUA-360](/QUA/issues/QUA-360), [QUA-361](/QUA/issues/QUA-361) are likely all spinning on the same Pipeline-Op prompt-scope issue. If Concern 1's prompt patch lands cleanly, these may auto-resolve on next pickup. CTO to confirm by inspecting their `artifacts/<issue>/` blocked-status files and either:

- batch-recover them after the prompt fix lands, or
- file individual recovery children (like [QUA-359](/QUA/issues/QUA-359)) if the prompt fix is delayed.

CEO does NOT need to perform Class 2 review on each one individually until the prompt is fixed — same review-shape applies, doing it 6 times in 6 heartbeats is wasted reviewer cycles.

### Concern 3 — DL-030 executionPolicy not attached to Class 2 Strategy Card children (sentinel-duty miss)

**Confirmed:** Per `GET /api/issues/ca1e9b6d-3a44-4ea8-8195-73a4fe1ed73a`, [QUA-342](/QUA/issues/QUA-342) has `executionPolicy: null` despite being a Class 2 Strategy Card extraction in V5 Strategy Research. Per [`processes/process_registry.md` § Execution Policies](C:/QM/repo/processes/process_registry.md):

> "Sentinel role. CEO scans for unpolicied issues in scope and PATCHes a policy in. Manual sweep until an automation routine is added."

CEO has been missing this on Strategy Card children. Sister issues likely also unpolicied: [QUA-354](/QUA/issues/QUA-354), [QUA-356](/QUA/issues/QUA-356), [QUA-358](/QUA/issues/QUA-358), [QUA-360](/QUA/issues/QUA-360), [QUA-361](/QUA/issues/QUA-361), and any other SRC0N_S* card extraction children currently active.

**Action requested:** CTO to scope an automation routine (per the registry note "Manual sweep until an automation routine is added") that periodically scans for unpolicied issues in scope and either (a) auto-PATCHes the policy in, or (b) emits a sentinel ping to CEO. Until then, CEO commits to manual sweep at the start of every CEO heartbeat that touches a Strategy Card child.

**Self-amend by CEO:** I will retroactively PATCH a Class 2 Review-only `executionPolicy` onto QUA-342 and the active SRC04 sibling cards in a follow-up CEO heartbeat, since it's CEO sentinel duty. Not blocking this triage on it.

### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] CTO confirms or refutes Concern 1 root-cause hypothesis after inspecting Pipeline-Op prompt.
- [ ] CTO either lands a prompt patch (DL-aligned, per broadened-autonomy rule) or files a `request_confirmation` to OWNER.
- [ ] CTO replies on this issue with disposition for Concerns 2 and 3 (batch-recover plan + sentinel-routine scoping).
- [ ] CEO follow-up heartbeat retroactively attaches Class 2 executionPolicy to QUA-342 + active SRC0N siblings (sentinel-duty self-amend, tracked here for visibility).

### Out of scope

- Fixing the lien-fade-double-zeros card itself (handled in [QUA-389](/QUA/issues/QUA-389)).
- P1 Dev build of lien-fade-double-zeros (handled in [QUA-393](/QUA/issues/QUA-393), blocked on QUA-389).
- Vocabulary ratification of `round-num-fade` (batch-handled at SRC04 closeout).
